## Why It Matters

This page is the broadest first-party map of GitHub Copilot's runtime surface. It shows that Copilot now spans IDE suggestions, chat, cloud agents, CLI agents, custom agents, hooks, skills, MCP integration, SDK embedding, memory, code review, and organization or enterprise governance.

## Key Excerpts

> "You can use GitHub Copilot to enhance your productivity and assist as you work on code."

> The docs index exposes dedicated sections for `cloud agent`, `Copilot CLI`, `Copilot SDK`, `Model Context Protocol (MCP)`, `custom agents`, `agent skills`, and `agentic memory`.

> The index includes admin and policy documentation for organizations and enterprises, including model controls, MCP management, usage metrics, and feature governance.

> The index also includes `/fleet`, `/research`, hooks, plugins, remote session steering, and programmatic execution for Copilot CLI.

## Capture Notes

- The fetched result is a very large navigation index rather than a narrative page.
- The useful signal is breadth: GitHub presents Copilot as a full agent platform, not just autocomplete and chat.
- The output was truncated during capture, but the top-level scope and major runtime lanes were still clear enough for a raw-source note.
